title: Verbs in context (A2)
lesson: Choose the verb that fits each situation. Watch out for pairs that are easy to confuse, like borrow and lend.

items.text: Can I {1} your pen? I will give it back tomorrow.
items.explanation: You borrow something that you take and later return.

items.text: I will {1} you ten pounds until you get paid.
items.explanation: You lend something that you give for someone to use and return.

items.text: Be careful not to {1} your keys again.
items.explanation: You lose something when you can no longer find it.

items.text: Our team played well and managed to {1} the match.
items.explanation: You win a match when you beat the other team.

items.text: We will {1} a flat in the city for the summer.
items.explanation: You rent a place by paying to use it for a time.

items.text: The mechanic will {1} my broken car tomorrow.
items.explanation: You repair something that is broken to make it work again.

items.text: I try to {1} some money each month for a holiday.
items.explanation: You save money when you keep it instead of using it.

items.text: He likes to {1} all his money on video games.
items.explanation: You spend money when you use it to buy things.

items.text: We want to {1} all our friends to the party.
items.explanation: You invite people when you ask them to come.

items.text: She went to {1} to the manager about the cold food.
items.explanation: You complain when you say you are not happy about something.

items.text: It is hard to {1} which film to watch tonight.
items.explanation: You decide when you choose between options.

items.text: I practise every day to {1} my English.
items.explanation: You improve something when you make it better.

items.text: Please {1} the rubbish bin before you leave.
items.explanation: You empty something by taking everything out of it.

items.text: Remember to {1} the milk in the fridge so it stays cold.
items.explanation: You keep something by storing it in a place.

items.text: The doctor told him to {1} smoking for his health.
items.explanation: You quit a habit when you stop doing it.

items.text: I need to {1} this form before I send it to the office.
items.explanation: You complete a form by filling in all of it.

items.text: Could you {1} the meaning of this word for me?
items.explanation: You explain something to make it clear and easy to understand.

items.text: We will {1} our holiday for next August.
items.explanation: You plan something by deciding how it will happen.

items.text: I want to {1} this jacket; do you have a bigger size?
items.explanation: You exchange an item by swapping it for another.

items.text: Please {1} this letter at the post office.
items.explanation: You post a letter by sending it through the post.

items.text: I will {1} a table at the restaurant for eight o'clock.
items.explanation: You book something by arranging to keep it for you.

items.text: Try not to {1} the bus; the next one is in an hour.
items.explanation: You miss a bus when you fail to catch it in time.

items.text: The two friends {1} to meet at the cinema later.
items.explanation: You agree when you and another person share the same idea.

items.text: I forgot to {1} my brother's birthday this year.
items.explanation: You remember something that you keep in your mind.

items.text: She wants to {1} a new language next year.
items.explanation: You learn a language when you gain the skill to use it.

items.text: The waiter will {1} our food to the table.
items.explanation: You bring something when you carry it to a place.

items.text: We should {1} early to avoid the traffic.
items.explanation: You leave a place when you go away from it.

items.text: Can you {1} me how to get to the station?
items.explanation: You tell someone something by giving them information.

items.text: He will {1} a speech at the wedding tonight.
items.explanation: You give a speech when you speak to a group of people.

items.text: The price of bread will {1} next month.
items.explanation: Something increases when it becomes larger or more.

items.text: I need to {1} my appointment because I am ill.
items.explanation: You cancel something to stop it from happening.

items.text: Please {1} the children while I cook dinner.
items.explanation: You watch people to look after and keep an eye on them.

items.text: We hope the weather will {1} before the weekend.
items.explanation: Something changes when it becomes different.

items.text: I want to {1} this old phone for some money.
items.explanation: You sell something by giving it to someone for money.
